nameMeanings.name: Wilson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Will + patronymic -son ‘son of Will’. Will was a very common medieval short form of William . This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Will
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Will, a short form of William . This surname is now commonly found in Scotland, in Aberdeenshire, Angus, and Kincardineshire.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring or stream, from Middle English wel, wel(l)e, wille, in a southwestern dialect form. This surname is found predominantly in the south and southwestern parts of the country. Compare Well 1.
English: nickname from Middle English iwil, wil(l)e ‘agreeable, pleasing’.

nameMeanings.name: Sue
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Susan   and, less commonly, of   Susanna   and   Suzanne .

variants.name: Su
meanings: Short form of   Susan   and, less commonly, of   Susanna   and   Suzanne .

variants.name: Soo
meanings: Recent coinage, a fanciful respelling of   Sue .

variants.name: Susan
meanings: vernacular form of   Susanna .  Among well-known bearers are the American film stars  Susan Hayward  (  1918–75 ) and  Susan Sarandon  (  b. 1946  as  Susan Tomalin ).

nameMeanings.name: Malinda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern coinage, derived from the first syllable of names such as Melanie and Melissa , with the addition of the productive suffix -inda (as in Lucinda ).

en.lifeSummary: Sue Malinda Wilson was born about 1420. She married John Hanks Sr about 1440, in Buckland, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters.
